programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. Drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien suffers a major dilemma - she wants to have children but is scared witless by the prospect of being pregnant. Meanwhile, Garth becomes a champion pancake tosser - much to his imprisoned father's dismay. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Richard tries to clinch a lucrative cosmetics deal with a glamorous French businesswoman, but when she propositions him, he lies that he has a wife. Now all he has to do is persuade Audrey to pose as Mrs DeVere for the weekend. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles, with a guest appearance by Rula Lenska.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo overhears Nora Batty discussing the effect Errol Flynn's riding outfit used to have on her, so he sets out to become the man of her dreams. Bill Owen stars.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The mummified remains of a woman are found during a rescue operation in a cave, and the body is discovered to be the ex-wife of a chief constable. Dalziel and Pascoe investigate, and their lives are put on the line when they brave treacherous conditions to uncover more evidence at the scene of the find. With Denis Lawson, Reece Dinsdale, Christine Tremarco and Sylivia Syms.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of a child's mutilated body in a river forces Leo to face a ghost from his past. Questions are raised as to whether the victim was murdered in a ritual killing, and evidence points the team in the direction of a nearby church. Meanwhile, Nikki investigates the apparently suspicious death of a priest from a local Catholic boys' school.(n)

Joining them in the kitchen are fish supplier Fiona Houston and food-and-drink journalist Rosalind Erskine, who's mixing an Angus breakfast martini using local gin infused with marmalade.(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including the FA Cup sixth-round ties and Premier League fixtures.(n)

programme.desc: Germany v England. Another chance to see the Euro 96 semi-final from Wembley Stadium.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Norwich City v Manchester United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Carrow Road. Norwich are struggling in the Premier League this season, but produced one of their best performances of the campaign to defeat Tottenham Hotspur on penalties away from home in the previous round. However, they face another tough task against the Red Devils, who are yet to concede a goal in the competition this term. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1990s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are Jayne Middlemiss, Diane Louise Jordan, Sarah Cawood, Jeremy Spake, Angus Loughran, Kriss Akabusi MBE, Philip Franks and Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. Tonight, he looks back on the hilarious Send To All's from singer Olly Murs and EastEnders' Danny Dyer, as well as the moment singer Peter Andre was awoken by Michael in the middle of the night to play the Midnight Gameshow.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer

Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. As Henry's home country prepares to host Euro 2016 following last November's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As organisations like the National Trust open up to visitors again, Ellie Harrison heads to Charlecote Park in Warwickshire, one of the great estates of the Elizabethan era. She's rolling up her sleeves to help with Britain's oldest managed flock of Jacob sheep, and visits a working Georgian water mill that's busier than ever, supplying the lockdown baking boom. Dwayne Fields investigates the challenges facing members of the BAME community living in the countryside, and Adam Henson is judging entrants in an online livestock show.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Newcastle United v Manchester City (Kick-off 6.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at St James' Park. This is Newcastle's first appearance at this stage in 14 years, but they are unbeaten in their last two matches against the Citizens here in the Premier League, drawing 2-2 this season and prevailing 2-1 last term. However, it remains to be seen how they will fare against Pep Guardiola's cup holders without the backing of their vociferous home support.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Erddig in North Wales. Items brought in to be valued include a mourning ring with a connection to Charlotte Bronte, a Welsh love spoon handed down through the generations, a jade figurine from the Summer Palace in Beijing, and an 18th-century wine bottle which has links to a Scottish artist.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: In 1865, Lydia is preparing to host a party at the fortune parlour, while Anna tells Crosbie that the key to the safe - which contains his gold - is missing, but he drinks from a spiked decanter and passes out before he can learn more. Alistair Lauderback arrives at the Godspeed to meet Lydia, only to find Carver, who blackmails him into handing over the ship. Period mystery based on the Eleanor Catton novel, starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie, Ewen Leslie and Marton Csokas.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Is the biggest mistake in the Covid crisis about to happen? As the country comes out of lockdown, the UK needs a test-and-trace system that can stop the disease from spreading again. So is that system ready to keep us safe? Reporter Richard Bilton investigates the rapid expansion of our testing capacity and asks whether we have got the world-beating service the Prime Minister promised.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley sits down for a chat with two of EastEnders' original cast members - Adam Woodyatt and Letitia Dean - AKA Ian Beale and Sharon Watts, who reflect on their careers. The pair reminisce about starting on the show together as teenagers, revisit classic scenes - and classic hair-dos - and share their best backstage stories. They also tease just what's in store for Ian and Sharon when the show returns.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: New performances of the writer's series of monologues continue with Her Big Chance, starring Jodie Comer. Actress Lesley prides herself on her professionalism - always getting involved up to the hilt, even if the job is just a walk-on. She tends to play fun-loving girls, although she's a very serious person. She's offered an audition by Spud, an interesting man she meets at a party. At the audition, after she has stripped down to bra and panties, she's given the part of Travis, who spends the film on the deck of a yacht.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: The classic monologues continue as Lucian Msamati performs Playing Sandwiches, the story of park attendant Wilfred. At the bandstand, there's a young mother with a child, seven-year-old Samantha, who takes a shine to him and gives him a daffodil - but it soon becomes clear why Wilfred's records cannot be traced.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: It's Terry's birthday and Arabella is looking forward to hosting a party in her honour, but nothing goes to plan for either of them. Kwame opens up to Terry about losing his virginity. Susy Henry is delighted to hear that Arabella has taken up her suggestion to write about her rape, but when Arabella asks for an early transfer of her delivery fee, Susy contends that would be tricky. Elsewhere, Theo confesses that she gets paid more to recruit people of colour. Drama, starring Michaela Coel and Weruche Opia.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Melvin Odoom and Naomi Wilkinson help a family of seven youngster plan their very own music festival wedding. Mum and dad think they ve got off easy until the paint begins to fly and they see their campervan assault course. However, with a surprise headline performance from Union J, it s looks this wedding could be a real smash hit.(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: This episode follows Rory at his first-ever music festival.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out a super-powered squirrel that can withstand the freezing temperatures of the Arctic winter.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The very best of the Main Events, including Kent's World Custard Pie Championships.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team presents a music special, talking about the songs they love, and Mwaksy Mudenda attempts a sing and sign-language challenge. Coldplay's Chris Martin, saxophonist YolanDa and chart star Anne Marie reveal how viewers can get involved and Ella Henderson performs her new single.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish.(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist concludes his journey by examining the effects of light interacting with the Earth, travelling to Iceland to witness a moonbow - the band of colours released when moonbeams are refracted by the spray of a waterfall. He then visits the site of an enormous outpouring of lava to explain why hot things glow red, and reveals how the different colours of light energy from the sun transform the plains of the Serengeti in Tanzania and drive the migration of humpback whales to the Caribbean each year.(n)

programme.title: Born in China (2016)
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary telling the stories of a panda and her cub, a young golden monkey and his sister and a mother snow leopard struggling to raise her two cubs in their homeland of China. Narrated by John Krasinski.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Over the past four weeks, 18 volunteers have experienced what life was like for the factory girls of the south Wales valleys in the 1960s, 1970s and 1980s. Now some of the women who lived through it for real talk about their lives, speaking of terrifying bosses, everyday sexism, union battles, camaraderie and of lifelong friendships forged on the production line. Presented by Alex Jones, who also reveals how the factory was brought back to life using these women's stories as inspiration.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip across Yorkshire, meeting fisherman Frank Powell near Bridlington and using some of his catch to cook baked sea trout with sun-dried tomatoes and lemons. She then heads to Huddersfield to sample local halloumi made by Syrian refugee Razan Alsous, creating watermelon and halloumi skewers. At home, Nadiya makes rhubarb and custard ice-cream sandwiches and Yorkshire puddings filled with strawberry jam and custard.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The second leg of Si King and Dave Myers' journey in search of Europe's finest baking takes them through the Low Countries. The Netherlands' heritage as a spice-trading nation shines through in such dishes as Dutch apple pie smothered in cinnamon, and Jewish buns coated in muscovado sugar. In Belgium, the duo make their own chocolate truffle cheesecake, and get busy again in Luxembourg, cooking a pork pie with Riesling jelly. Along the way they snort chocolate, taste the sweet delights of Bruges and get a recipe for macaroons with ice-cream.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final stage as the last three contestants take on a trio of celebration wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young start by challenging the sewers to make a little boys kilt without a pattern. In the Transformation Challenge, they each attempt to turn party dresses into fun outfits fit to wear at a carnival, before finally creating unique gowns using luxury fabric in the Made to Measure challenge. At the end of the three challenges, one of the competitors is crowned this year's winner.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades visits the recycling centre in Altrincham in search of three pieces of skip-bound trash that he can rework and repurpose. Jay enlists lighting expert Guy Trench to give new life to an old weighing scale, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell's creativity is pushed to the limit with a rusty wheelbarrow. Jay's own skills are tested restoring a baptismal font cover. Narrated by Arthur Smith.(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Downeys sample a week in Brisbane to decide whether husband Andrew's Australian dream could become a reality. But any such move would mean leaving eldest daughter Sarah behind.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents Escape to the Country highlights, focusing on older properties such as thatched cottages, timber-framed houses and mansion apartments.(n)

programme.title: Cricket: England v West Indies Greatest Matches
programme.desc: A look back at the Second Test of the 2000 series, which came from Lord's - match which saw bowlers largely come out on top.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from around the country, visiting Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon. Items range from a signed photo of Elvis to a stick-back chair, while Paul Martin explores the treasures and lavish interiors of Parham House in West Sussex.(n)

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Storm Hector causes havoc on the tracks, Sajid completes his last shift before he retires after 45 years in the station, and Beyonce and Jay-Z are in town.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: Rick's journey draws to a close as he heads east from Oaxaca to the Yucatan Peninsula, where dishes like Papadzules and Sikil Pak are enjoyed in villages across the countryside. Here, locals feast on the hottest chilli of them all, the Habanero. Rick ends his journey feasting on grilled seafood in Tulum, along the shores of the Caribbean Sea.(n)

programme.title: The Glastonbury Experience Live
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Lauren Laverne present highlights from Glastonbury's musical vaults, on what would have been the festival's 50th anniversary. Moments include Bruce Springsteen's 2009 visit, the Arctic Monkeys' second time headlining in 2013 and Dizzee Rascal's Pyramid Stage collaboration with Florence and the Machine in 2010. Plus, Janelle Monae ruling the West Holts stage with her catchy hits in 2011 and Coldplay's debut on the Other Stage back in 2000.(n)

programme.title: Adele at Glastonbury 2016
programme.desc: As part of televised celebrations across the weekend marking what would have been 50 years of the Glastonbury Festival, tonight's programme features London-born singer-songwriter Adele's headline appearance on the world famous Pyramid Stage in June 2016, her second appearance at the festival following her small-scale debut on the Park Stage in 2007. Here, she showcased her superstar status with hits including Hello, Rumour Has It, Skyfall, Rolling in the Deep, Make You Feel and Someone Like You.(n)

programme.title: Coldplay at Glastonbury 2016
programme.desc: Coldplay closed Glastonbury 2016 with a bang, bringing their powerful and emotive pop anthems to the Pyramid Stage. Playing hits including Fix You, Paradise and Yellow, they were also joined onstage by Barry Gibb for renditions of Bee Gees classics To Love Somebody and Stayin' Alive. The band also paid tribute to Viola Beach, a young rock band (discovered via BBC Music Introducing) whose members and manager died in a car accident in Sweden just a few months earlier.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey of dishes, with this episode's recipes linked by the letter P. The Hairy Bikers cook paella and Nigel Slater bakes plum pudding cake.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani visits Epping Forest to find out about its past, its wildlife and to meet some of the people who look after it. She also discovers the art of 'cattle whispering' when she meets the herd of longhorns that help to conserve the Forest and, at dusk, she's on the trail of one of the UK's rarest creatures. Wildlife film-maker Hamza Yassin gets up close to a much-loved seabird, the puffin, near his home on the west coast of Scotland, while Gareth Wyn Jones offers a glimpse into his life as a hill farmer in Wales. Down on his own farm, Adam Henson has a shearing dilemma to solve.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don shows how to prune spring-flowering roses and makes successional sowings of vegetables to ensure continuous crops. Adam Frost gives tips helping plants cope with drought conditions and shows how to save precious water. The programme meets two sisters from Dorset who have discovered a new love of gardening, supported in their efforts by many followers on social media, and in Bognor Regis visits a gardener who has used unusual materials and bargain plants to create her own dream garden. Plus, Frances Tophill heads to Hertfordshire to meet a couple of brothers whose gardens are designed with wildlife in mind and in.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn are in their kitchens cooking with Scottish produce. Euan McIlwraith reflects on how the bird of prey population has changed over the time he's been reporting and Arlene Stuart investigates the impact of Covid-19 on the traditional Common Riding Festivals.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson shows how easy it is to fix a fallen fig, Kirsty Wilson explores the unique Rain Garden at the Royal Botanic Garden in Edinburgh. Agony plant aunt Carole Baxter is back to answer more viewers' homegrown questions.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix travels to Austria's capital Vienna in the company of food writer Rachel Khoo. The duo begin in the city's first district, the epicentre of coffee house culture, a crucial part of the Viennese social scene. Here, Rachel takes Fred to the iconic Café Demel, known for its exquisite cakes. Moving on, they learn how to make a spinach, cream and bread dumpling, before meeting a trailblazing chef taking the city by storm. Fred in particular is wowed by his schnitzel, reworked into a haute cuisine masterpiece. Finally the duo end their evening with a visit to one of the city's many sausage stands.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The third leg of Si King and Dave Myers' biking-and-baking tour takes them to Germany, where they discover people have a passion for bread in every shape and size. The duo turn out potato bread with asparagus soup, and also rustle up bierocks - meat-filled rolls - to accompany a stein of beer, and their own version of the classic Black Forest gateau.(n)

programme.title: World Beaters
programme.desc: Alicia Keys tells the story of her friend, tennis legend Serena Williams.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: A look back at last year's tournament at the All England Club, which saw Novak Djokovic and Simona Halep emerge as the men's and women's singles champions.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. The first episode focuses on frontline workers, all of whom have a courageous story to tell.(n)

programme.title: This Beautiful Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman who dreams of being a children's author forges an unlikely friendship with a cantankerous, rich old widower, as well as his housekeeper and an inventor who frequents the library where she works. Drama, starring Jessica Brown Findlay, Tom Wilkinson, Andrew Scott and Jeremy Irvine.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The final episode of the documentary features a tour of an extraordinary new home - Villa Troglodyte, which has come onto the market. Built as if carved out of the rock, the price is 'only' £23million because it doesn't have a sea view. The pressure to build more houses in such a small country has left Prince Albert with a challenge. Cameras follow him as he attends meetings about a huge two-billion-euro land extension project he has initiated. Extending into the sea is a complex operation, but once complete the largest villas there are estimated to sell for around 100million euros.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace checks in with one of Britain's biggest biscuit factories, chatting to some of the workers via video calls to find out how they are coping during the coronavirus pandemic. Gregg speaks to manager Nina Sparks, whom he met during his visit back in 2017. Nina reassures him that all the ingredients are still getting through, including the flour that has been missing from many supermarket shelves due to a packaging shortage. Nina agrees to be Gregg's 'roving reporter', to show him what's changed on the production lines to meet demand, and what new safety measures have been put in place to keep the staff safe.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury Tea Time Legends
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe and Jo Whiley introduce some of the best performances from the Glastonbury Festival's regular Sunday evening legends slot, including Kylie Minogue's acclaimed set from the 2019 festival. Other artists featured include Neil Diamond, Brian Wilson, Shirley Bassey, Lionel Richie and Al Green.(n)

programme.title: The Glastonbury Experience Live
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Lauren Laverne introduce highlights from classic performances at the Glastonbury Festival, including previously unseen footage. The programme includes Nick Cave and the Bad Seeds playing the penultimate slot in 1999, classic headline concerts from REM in 2003 and Blur in 2009, Lady Gaga's one and only Glastonbury appearance from 2015, and Mary J Blige thrilling a rain-soaked crowd in 2015.(n)

programme.title: David Bowie at Glastonbury 2000
programme.desc: Highlights of the singer's headline set on the Pyramid Stage that closed the festival on Sunday 25 June 2000, regarded as one of his greatest live performances since the 1970s. Only the first five songs and the encore were broadcast on the original TV coverage, although the entire set was captured on film. This re-edited version includes previously unbroadcast performances of hits including Ashes to Ashes, Starman and Let's Dance.(n)

programme.title: Ed Sheeran at Glastonbury 2017
programme.desc: Another chance to see Ed Sheeran's performance at the Pyramid Stage, which marked the singer's debut as a Glastonbury headliner. The set features hits Shape of You, Castle on the Hill and Thinking Out Loud.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from viewers at home. Presented by Fiona Bruce.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: An urgent call is made by a man whose wife is in cardiac arrest, with a doctor and paramedic team from North West Air Ambulance immediately dispatched to assist on the job. Back in control, a call is in progress for a patient suffering chest pains as a result of cocaine binge. Meanwhile out on the road, crewmates Chris and V are dispatched to a category-three call for a patient who's fallen. However, once the patient is safely treated the couple's dogs become the focus of the paramedics' attention. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's surgery is derailed when Evie reveals a secret that makes him question himself, but both suspect they are just making excuses out of a fear of what the future may hold. Nicky treats a familiar face, and is put in a difficult position by her mother that forces her to choose between cutting her off or making a promise she cannot keep. Max's big day is ruined by devastating revelations from three of her colleagues.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson focuses on the historic county of Yorkshire, taking a look back at some of the team's adventures away from the house hunts. Clips showcase the county's manufacturing traditions, food, adrenaline sports and pastimes.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon lend their antiques expertise to two teams in Lewes, East Sussex, as they compete to make a profit at auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion also pays a visit to the nearby Brighton Toy Museum.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull explore their favourite inventions and scientific breakthroughs from the years between 1850 and 1900, beginning at Crossness Pumping Station in south-east London, which was part of the redevelopment of London's sewerage system. They then head to London Zoo, where they get their hands on a first edition of Charles Darwin's masterwork On the Origin of Species.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament.(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of My Family
programme.desc: Documentary charting 200 years of social history by exploring four different family trees. The project started off with a number of upper-class residents and slum dwellers from Victorian Britain, then traced them forward to learn their stories, ultimately hearing first-hand from their living descendants. The first edition focuses on a trio of pickpocketing sisters raised in 1830s Shoreditch, two of whom were banished to Australia. Their descendants include two Supreme Court judges, a bin man and a window cleaner.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen visit Dorset and sample food from Dorchester, where they find out about some of the famous names associated with the town. They also dine at the courtroom of the hanging judge, Judge Jeffreys, and sample pie and chips at the old architect's office used by Thomas Hardy. Their pursuit for the best local food takes them to a garlic farm, a chocolate factory and a vicar who supplies the local cider.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind
programme.desc: Roger Federer v Rafael Nadal. Clare Balding presents action from the 2008 men's singles final at the All England Club, widely regarded as one of the finest matches in history.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott takes Len Goodman on a road trip around Britain to expand the Strictly Come Dancing judge's culinary horizons. The pair begin in Manchester, where Len has his first taste of Chinese food and samples some Mexican mezcal, while Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a stir-fried beef dish inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Adam Partridge value items brought to Muncaster Castle, near Ravenglass in Cumbria, while Paul Martin finds out about the Lake District's bobbin industry.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman revisits memorable country houses with contemporary features, where natural materials have been combined with modern building techniques to create a new generation of rural properties. There's a look back at the evolution of the kitchen, and highlights from visits to forward-thinking rural businesses.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. In the first edition, Fred heads to Venice in the company of chef-restaurateur Angela Hartnett whose Italian family heritage has given her a lifelong love of Italian food. She takes him enjoy the simple but stunning food at her favourite restaurant Osteria Alle Testiere and the unforgettable service at Caffè Florian - one of the oldest cafes in Europe.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review. The programme also takes a look behind the scenes and into the inner sanctum of one of the most beautiful sporting venues in the world.(n)

programme.title: Italy's Frontline: A Doctor's Diary
programme.desc: Documentary featuring an unprecedented and intimate insight into three months of life on the Covid frontline in Italy with A&E doctor Francesca Mangiatordi. In the early days of the outbreak, the hospital is overflowing and Francesa works gruelling 12-hour shifts caring for those who have been most affected. Due to a lack of life-saving equipment she faces the traumatising task of prioritising which lives to try to save. At home, Francesca is fearful of infecting her husband, who suffers from a respiratory condition, and she hasn't touched him - or her children - for more than a month. Narrated by Florence Pugh.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: More comedians perform a selection of quickfire sketches, songs and isolation anecdotes, filmed by themselves in and around their own homes during the coronavirus lockdown. Russell Kane rants about the supermarket frontline, the trio behind hit podcast My Dad Wrote a Porno offer tips on how to write erotica, and Mackenzie Crook brings Mr Bagshaw back to life in his bedroom. Plus, skits featuring London Hughes and Rosie Jones.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The second of two editions celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Tom Allen, Stephen K Amos, Jen Brister, Al Porter, Julian Clary, Suzi Ruffell and Gina Yashere.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Filmmaker James Jones conducts a forensic investigation into the death of William Chapman, the black teenager who was shot dead in Portsmouth, Virginia, by white police officer Stephen Rankin on April 22, 2015. As a result of the incident, Rankin was fired from Portsmouth Police Department, and was charged with first-degree murder, but Jones reveals that Chapman was only one of 306 black men killed by police in the US last year, and only 14 officers have faced charges. Making use of access to both prosecution and defence lawyers, as well as to both Rankin and Chapman's family and friends, Jones documents the unfolding drama of Rankin's trial, and sets his story against the wider socio-political conflict causing tension between black communities and the authorities across America today.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury Backstage Acoustics
programme.desc: The second of three compilations of the many impromptu, stripped-down performances that have graced the BBC Two presentation tent since coverage began back in 1997. Pop stars, legendary acts and breakthrough artists have all been featured over the years, giving audiences the chance to see artists from stages the cameras don't always cover.(n)

programme.title: Toots & The Maytals at Glastonbury 2010
programme.desc: The ska group's performance on the West Holts Stage, featuring songs such as Pressure Drop, Louie Louie, Monkey Man and 54-46 Was My Number. Introduced by Mark Radcliffe.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: The detective attempts to navigate local gossip in Vigata when posters appear around the town questioning the moral status of one of its female inhabitants. However, when a murder takes place, the resulting investigation unearths some unsavoury facts about the victim. Drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: The Chemical Brothers at Glastonbury 2000
programme.desc: The dance duo's Friday night headline performance on the Pyramid Stage at the iconic festival. Featuring hits including Hey Boy Hey Girl, Block Rockin' Beats and Setting Sun.(n)

programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 2000
programme.desc: Highlights of the summer festival, which saw thousands of music fans swarm to the legendary site to see acts including David Bowie, Travis, Pet Shop Boys and Nine Inch Nails.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on November 9, featuring performances by Janet Jackson, Electribe 101, Lisa Stansfield, Aaron Neville and Linda Ronstadt, and the Wonder Stuff.(n)

programme.title: K-Pop Idols: Inside the Hit Factory
programme.desc: The K-Pop phenomenon is shaking up the pop world and for the first time ever, there is now a serious challenge to the West's domination of the global music industry. James Ballardie travels to South Korea to uncover the secrets behind this worldwide success story, finding out how, in just 20 years, the music industry in the country has come from obscurity to become a major player on the world stage.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Following the efforts being made by scientists, conservationists and local people in the Highlands as they work to repair the damage of the past and rebuild wild landscapes and habitats. The programme meets Dave Anderson from Forestry Commission Scotland and his colleague Simon Smith as they tag and monitor red kite and osprey chicks, gaining a unique perspective of the lives of the birds before they fly the nest. Cameras also capture the efforts of the Scottish Wildcat Action Plan team, as well as Paul Lister's project to reintroduce wolves and bears to his Alladale Wilderness Reserve.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a pioneering team in Brazil as they track individual wild jaguars in order to gain new insights into their lives. However, when two young cubs are orphaned, the team must use its new-found knowledge to help them return to the wild.(n)

programme.title: Nile Rodgers & Chic at Glastonbury 2017
programme.desc: A chance to see the set from the Pyramid Stage in 2017, as Nile Rodgers & Chic went through a back catalogue that included We Are Family, Le Freak, Good Times and Get Lucky.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury Backstage Acoustics
programme.desc: The third and final compilation of the many impromptu, stripped-down performances that have graced the BBC Two presentation tent since coverage began back in 1997. Pop stars, legendary acts and breakthrough artists have all been featured over the years, giving audiences the chance to see artists from stages the cameras don't always cover.(n)

programme.title: Amy Winehouse at Glastonbury 2007
programme.desc: A look back to 2007, when Amy Winehouse performed twice in one day at the festival, featuring classic tracks such as Tears Dry On Their Own, Back To Black and Rehab.(n)

programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 2011
programme.desc: Highlights of the festival held at Worthy Farm in Somerset, which was headlined by U2, Coldplay and Beyonce. Other artists taking to the site's many stages across the weekend included Californian rockers Queens of the Stone Age, former Smiths frontman Morrissey, Mercury Prize-winning group Elbow and legendary guitarist BB King.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a pioneering team in Brazil as they track individual wild jaguars in order to gain new insights into their lives. However, when two young cubs are orphaned, the team must use its new-found knowledge to help them return to the wild.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat is stunned to see Bianca at her door and is shocked by the state she is in. Phil convinces Shirley to collect the results of her biopsy, and Gus meets the girl of his dreams.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca has plenty to say when Ricky turns up at Pat's, and Jack realises who has been dealing in the club. Gus is surprised by the arrival of his Aunt Opal, and Vinnie returns.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca moves in with Pat in a desperate attempt to get her kids back from social services, Roxy risks a rendezvous with Sean, and Gus gets closer to Keisha.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca waits nervously for the social worker, who is due to inspect Pat's house, Sean stirs up trouble for Gus and Keisha, while Ben keeps his new pastime a secret from Phil.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Alice Levine revisits three books by working class writers from the East Midlands - Sons and Lovers, Saturday Night and Sunday Morning, and The Secret Diary of Adrian Mole.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a fascinating scene of the sun dropping below the horizon.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga continue his exploration of black British history. Focusing on the Victorian era, David explores the moral crusade against slavery within this period of time and key moments that helped to shift the position of slavery within Britain. He discovers about mill workers of Rochdale that stood in solidarity with enslaved Africans in the American South, how Queen Victoria came to have a black god-daughter, and looks back at the victims of a tragedy in Jamaica.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed goes back to when Persia faced its gravest threat - Genghis Khan - and reveals how the Safavids, who succeeded the Mongols, give Iran a powerful new identity.(n)

programme.title: The Lady in the Van (2015)
programme.desc: The true story of writer Alan Bennett's friendship with an eccentric homeless woman, whom he befriended in the 1970s before allowing her to park her dilapidated Bedford van outside his Camden home, where she ended up staying for 15 years. Comedy-drama, starring Maggie Smith, Alex Jennings, Roger Allam and Jim Broadbent.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Diaries
programme.desc: A candid profile of the writer, following him in his home village in Yorkshire, to his community library in Primrose Hill and during a trip to New York. In a series of intimate encounters filmed over the period of a year, he reflects on his modest beginnings and his enduring gratitude to a Welfare State that paid for his education and looked after his parents in their old age.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Norwich City v Manchester United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Carrow Road. Norwich are struggling in the Premier League this season, but produced one of their best performances of the campaign to defeat Tottenham Hotspur on penalties away from home in the previous round. However, they face another tough task against the Red Devils, who are yet to concede a goal in the competition this term. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer

This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. As Henry's home country prepares to host Euro 2016 following last November's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As organisations like the National Trust open up to visitors again, Ellie Harrison heads to Charlecote Park in Warwickshire, one of the great estates of the Elizabethan era. She's rolling up her sleeves to help with Britain's oldest managed flock of Jacob sheep, and visits a working Georgian water mill that's busier than ever, supplying the lockdown baking boom. Dwayne Fields investigates the challenges facing members of the BAME community living in the countryside, and Adam Henson is judging entrants in an online livestock show.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Newcastle United v Manchester City (Kick-off 6.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at St James' Park. This is Newcastle's first appearance at this stage in 14 years, but they are unbeaten in their last two matches against the Citizens here in the Premier League, drawing 2-2 this season and prevailing 2-1 last term. However, it remains to be seen how they will fare against Pep Guardiola's cup holders without the backing of their vociferous home support.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Erddig in North Wales. Items brought in to be valued include a mourning ring with a connection to Charlotte Bronte, a Welsh love spoon handed down through the generations, a jade figurine from the Summer Palace in Beijing, and an 18th-century wine bottle which has links to a Scottish artist.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: In 1865, Lydia is preparing to host a party at the fortune parlour, while Anna tells Crosbie that the key to the safe - which contains his gold - is missing, but he drinks from a spiked decanter and passes out before he can learn more. Alistair Lauderback arrives at the Godspeed to meet Lydia, only to find Carver, who blackmails him into handing over the ship. Period mystery based on the Eleanor Catton novel, starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie, Ewen Leslie and Marton Csokas.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: New performances of the writer's series of monologues continue with Her Big Chance, starring Jodie Comer. She tends to play fun-loving girls, although she's a very serious person. She's offered an audition by Spud, an interesting man she meets at a party. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As organisations like the National Trust open up to visitors again, Ellie Harrison heads to Charlecote Park in Warwickshire, one of the great estates of the Elizabethan era. She's rolling up her sleeves to help with Britain's oldest managed flock of Jacob sheep, and visits a working Georgian water mill that's busier than ever, supplying the lockdown baking boom. Dwayne Fields investigates the challenges facing members of the BAME community living in the countryside, and Adam Henson is judging entrants in an online livestock show.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Newcastle United v Manchester City (Kick-off 6.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at St James' Park. This is Newcastle's first appearance at this stage in 14 years, but they are unbeaten in their last two matches against the Citizens here in the Premier League, drawing 2-2 this season and prevailing 2-1 last term. However, it remains to be seen how they will fare against Pep Guardiola's cup holders without the backing of their vociferous home support.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Erddig in North Wales. Items brought in to be valued include a mourning ring with a connection to Charlotte Bronte, a Welsh love spoon handed down through the generations, a jade figurine from the Summer Palace in Beijing, and an 18th-century wine bottle which has links to a Scottish artist.(n)

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: In 1865, Lydia is preparing to host a party at the fortune parlour, while Anna tells Crosbie that the key to the safe - which contains his gold - is missing, but he drinks from a spiked decanter and passes out before he can learn more. Alistair Lauderback arrives at the Godspeed to meet Lydia, only to find Carver, who blackmails him into handing over the ship. Period mystery based on the Eleanor Catton novel, starring Eva Green, Eve Hewson, Benedict Hardie, Ewen Leslie and Marton Csokas.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Is the biggest mistake in the Covid crisis about to happen? As the country comes out of lockdown, the UK needs a test-and-trace system that can stop the disease from spreading again. So is that system ready to keep us safe? Reporter Richard Bilton investigates the rapid expansion of our testing capacity and asks whether we have got the world-beating service the Prime Minister promised.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley sits down for a chat with two of EastEnders' original cast members - Adam Woodyatt and Letitia Dean - AKA Ian Beale and Sharon Watts, who reflect on their careers. The pair reminisce about starting on the show together as teenagers, revisit classic scenes - and classic hair-dos - and share their best backstage stories. They also tease just what's in store for Ian and Sharon when the show returns.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: New performances of the writer's series of monologues continue with Her Big Chance, starring Jodie Comer. Actress Lesley prides herself on her professionalism - always getting involved up to the hilt, even if the job is just a walk-on. She tends to play fun-loving girls, although she's a very serious person. She's offered an audition by Spud, an interesting man she meets at a party. At the audition, after she has stripped down to bra and panties, she's given the part of Travis, who spends the film on the deck of a yacht.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: The classic monologues continue as Lucian Msamati performs Playing Sandwiches, the story of park attendant Wilfred. At the bandstand, there's a young mother with a child, seven-year-old Samantha, who takes a shine to him and gives him a daffodil - but it soon becomes clear why Wilfred's records cannot be traced.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: It's Terry's birthday and Arabella is looking forward to hosting a party in her honour, but nothing goes to plan for either of them. Kwame opens up to Terry about losing his virginity. Susy Henry is delighted to hear that Arabella has taken up her suggestion to write about her rape, but when Arabella asks for an early transfer of her delivery fee, Susy contends that would be tricky. Elsewhere, Theo confesses that she gets paid more to recruit people of colour. Drama, starring Michaela Coel and Weruche Opia.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Anna Haugh and Ollie Dabbous, and guest Rebecca Front joins in via video-link. Plus, a mix of archive clips and wine choices by Olly Smith.(n)

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on Scottish fish, cooking a fish pie with mussels, haddock, plaice and smoked salmon. Joining them in the kitchen are fish supplier Fiona Houston and food-and-drink journalist Rosalind Erskine, who's mixing an Angus breakfast martini using local gin infused with marmalade.(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including the FA Cup sixth-round ties and Premier League fixtures.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs

This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.desc: The northern Italian region of Lombardy saw the first major Coronavirus outbreak in Europe. programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Alasdair comes back home to Màiri and Anna and they begin to get to know their 'new' mother-in-law and granny, Iseabail. He is pleasantly distracted with the arrival of his wife Sarah Jane, back from the city with her niece Isla. programme.title: The Road to Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jane Horrocks and Jessie Wallace star in a one-off drama telling the story of how the long-running soap was born. Tony Warren joins producer Harry Elton and director Derek Bennett as they struggle to create the programme, faced with opposition from ITV's formidable bosses Sidney and Cecil Bernstein, while Margaret Morris begins the hunt for the show's cast. With David Dawson, Stephen Berkoff, Celia Imrie and Lynda Baron.(n)

programme.title: The British Soap Awards
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts this celebration of the only TV awards ceremony dedicated entirely to soap as it reaches its 21st anniversary. He looks back at some of the most memorable winners, including the soap icons who have scooped the outstanding achievement award, the issue-led storylines that have swept the board, spectacular scenes and the big laughs along the way. He also looks at some of the soap double acts, such as Roy and Hayley, Kat and Alfie, and not forgetting the super-villains, including Corrie's 'Norman Bates with a briefcase' Richard Hillman and EastEnders' murderous bride Janine Butcher.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher and mass strikes leading to the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.title: The British Soap Awards
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts this celebration of the only TV awards ceremony dedicated entirely to soap as it reaches its 21st anniversary. He looks back at some of the most memorable winners, including the soap icons who have scooped the outstanding achievement award, the issue-led storylines that have swept the board, spectacular scenes and the big laughs along the way. He also looks at some of the soap double acts, such as Roy and Hayley, Kat and Alfie, and not forgetting the super-villains, including Corrie's 'Norman Bates with a briefcase' Richard Hillman and EastEnders' murderous bride Janine Butcher.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher and mass strikes leading to the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville.(n)

programme.desc: Tracy persuades a reluctant Leanne that they should have a family day out at the petting zoo as it would do Oliver good. Daniel heads to a hotel where he meets Nicky, Carla sets about decorating the Rovers for VE Day, and Seb plucks up the courage to tell Alina that he still has feelings for her.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David is amused when Shona steals Ray's wallet and explains that is how they met, while Daniel opens up to Nicky and tells her about Sinead. Leanne's thrilled to see a flash of the old Oliver at a petting zoo, and Peter is offended when Carla accuses him of beating up Jordan and Chelsey.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne is shocked that Steve has booked a family trip to Cornwall, and David is stunned when Shona leans in for a kiss and suggests they have sex. Toyah joins Evelyn and Nina on their protest, while Seb and Alina agree to meet for a drink as mates.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Everyone is shocked when a mystery woman arrives, claiming to be Ed's wife and seeking a reconciliation with him. However, a misunderstanding leaves her believing that Rosie is her estranged husband's new love interest. Meanwhile, a shocking link is made between some seemingly unrelated animal deaths. Jill Halfpenny (EastEnders, Waterloo Road) joins the cast.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: Coronation Street
