programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Master mechanic Fuzz Townshend and petrolhead Tim Shaw return to rescue more classic cars, beginning by working on a 1997 Lotus Elise belonging to a cancer patient who had hoped to restore the vehicle with her father.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw plan a surprise restoration of a 1972 Peugeot 504 cabriolet in need of a major overhaul - but nearly get caught smuggling it back to their workshop.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a 1962 Triumph TR4 belonging to a man left unable to drive it by a stroke, and it turns out be one of the biggest jobs they have ever taken on.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks compete for the £1,000 prize in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and keen basketball player Ken Oputa is the first to host, inviting his guests to turn up in sportswear fancy dress. He hopes his Double Dribble mushrooms and Slam Dunk salmon will prove to be winning ingredients.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer Erika Sager hosts the second dinner party of the week on board her boat in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and invites her guests to dress up as pirates. Despite the unconventional setting, her rivals are all impressed by the standard of her food and hospitality.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-two Sarah Brice hosts the third dinner party in Kingston upon Thames, hoping to impress her guests with her own take on dishes including prawns, lamb and chocolate mousse. Ken Oputa tries to make an impression on the female contestants with his romantic poetry, but finds Erika Sager switches her attentions to his rival Jamie Baker.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Jamie Baker hosts the fourth party in London, and his ambitious menu of langoustine and crab ravioli, herb crusted rack of lamb, and milloux of chocolate - combined with his choice of evening entertainment - go down well, until an unexpected turn of events scuppers his plans.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired golfer David Witcher hosts the final evening in Kingston upon Thames, south-west London, and hopes to impress with an American-themed menu, including Wyoming slow-cooked ribs and Louisiana seafood chowder. After dinner, he entertains his guests with a bucking bronco, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, B&B veteran Paul Workman, who runs Hamlet House in Stratford-upon-Avon, makes the most of his town's Shakespeare connection as another round of the competition gets under way - but his guests cause a fuss when a hair is found in the mushrooms at breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to B&B novices Ben Gray and Katie Keegan's Ashlack business in Cumbria. The guests admire rooms in the estate's 17th-century manor house, but Veronica Pickles and Saida Hammoud are unimpressed when they discover their accommodation is a yurt in the woods. Filled with dread at the idea of a midnight trip to the toilet facilities, they later make plain their feelings when checking out.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Richwood in Torquay, where partners Veronica Pickles and Saida Hammoud run the 13-bedroom guest house by themselves. The diners are given a crash course in belly-dancing and provided with a feast of Lebanese delicacies - but are soon distracted by the dated decor.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Helen Fraser welcomes her guests to the Tai'r Bull Inn in the Brecon Beacons, with moral support from her friend Kath. Ben and Katie are quick to praise the quality of their lodgings, but B&B veteran Paul spots a few mistakes, while Saida and Veronica refuse to spend the night in their room after they make a discovery.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. A clash then ensues between two of the week's hosts before the winner of the competition is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Driving instructor Ray Dodd hosts the first dinner party in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, but finds his guests a difficult crowd to please. Recruitment executive Michelle Watson turns her nose up at the salmon starter, while choir director Joseph Benneh sends his steak back to the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment executive Michelle Watson welcomes the diners to her home for the second dinner party of the week in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, but the previous night's host Ray Dodd is hungry for revenge following stinging criticism of his efforts. Michelle's cooking also fails to impress Joseph Benneh, so her hopes of scooping the week's prize rest with her choice of entertainment.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Stuart Henson hosts a safari-themed dinner party on the third night in Milton Keynes. He hopes his exotic menu of zebra steak and 'elephant dung surprise' will please his guests, but after the insults of the previous night he faces a difficult challenge to persuade his fellow diners to get along.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Choir director Joseph Benneh hosts the fourth dinner party in Milton Keynes, hoping the flavours from his homeland Ghana and a vocal masterclass will prove a winning combination. The guests' expectations are high, so he is under pressure to hit the spot with a menu that includes chicken liver pate, and groundnut soup with coconut rice.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Country enthusiast Susan Rogers hosts the final dinner party in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, hoping her French cuisine will win her extra points. She also demonstrates her skills with an accordion and gives her guests an origami masterclass, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Sarah and Dean Berry, who returned to live in their home town of Newport, Gwent, after 17 years in London. The couple have bought an 18th-century folly - a castle perched on a hilltop - and plan to restore the building to its former glory, while also wrapping a large modern extension around the original structure.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Major road traffic collisions that paramedics have been faced with, including a quad biker who has collided with the windscreen of a car on a residential street in Birmingham.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man suffers serious spinal injuries after falling off a mountain bike. As doctors assess the extent of the damage, his wife recalls growing up in apartheid-era South Africa. A medical team gives emergency oxygen to an 83-year-old woman who is struggling to breathe, and a woman is rushed to A&E after her daughter accidently crashed into a lamp post.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the work of police in Northamptonshire, where, with resources stretched to breaking point, officers are struggling to meet the increased demand on their services. This edition looks at the impact of relationships on the force, with last year seeing were more than 13,000 domestic incidents in the county.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Yanni's in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter Avgoustiou, a chef so set in his ways that he hasn't allowed any changes to his menu since the Greek restaurant opened in 1984. Peter's family is concerned the business is becoming trapped in the past, and standards are slipping, but he seems determined to stick to his guns.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Major road traffic collisions that paramedics have been faced with, including a quad biker who has collided with the windscreen of a car on a residential street in Birmingham.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular winter sun destinations. Potential buyers explore the beautiful La Cala de Mijas, Spain.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items languishing in people's homes and use creative thinking and elbow grease to fix them up to turn a profit for their owners. Here they unearth a church pew, create a fish tank from scrap and uncover a special petrol tank.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in East Sussex and Hertfordshire, with finds that include a Victorian egg incubator.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Andy and Mandy Boylett, who plan to build a huge bungalow in Yarm, North Yorkshire, complete with a basement and sunken garden, as they pursue their dream of living mortgage-free. If they can stick to their budget, they could save more than £130,000 - but the build soon becomes more disaster zone than dream home as the couple battle one problem after another.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at North Bridge Street B&B in Hawick in the Scottish Borders, which is run by Eileen Walsh and her Portuguese partner Emanuel Forte. When the guests arrive, the purple-themed rooms cause a reaction, while one competitor's constant criticising irritates another.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at the Golden Fleece in Ruleholme, Cumbria, run by chef Robert Cowan and his partner, Sylvia Spark. Before long, guests Eileen and Emmanuel pick up on cleanliness issues, and breakfast proves to be a disappointment.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Bubble Inn in Stenson, Derbyshire for the third day of the competition, where unsightly stains and security leave some of them less than impressed. Manager and ex-semi-professional boxer Connie Eleftheriou is keen to take his rivals in the ring with him, however the real damage happens when Emmanuel is taken to hospital after some overenthusiastic dancing at a Greek-themed night.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Melinda Beckett-Hughes is the final host of the week at Ayuda House in Greater Manchester - and as she has spent the week picking fault, the guests expect perfection from her. However, Robert and Sylvia are shocked to find a dirty towel in a drawer, while Emmanuel and Eileen cannot believe the levels of dust and Connie is horrified by the carpet.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some old scores. Eileen and Emmanuel from North Bridge Street want to get to the bottom of Melinda and Stewart's dirty bin comments as well as challenging Melinda's shower issues. Robert and Sylvia from the Golden Fleece try to defend their breakfast disaster, but also pull Eileen and Emmanuel up on their low score.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team visits Swindon and Chippenham, where Henry Cole finds and restores a much-maligned BSA Ariel 3 moped and Simon O'Brien gives a Ford Transit gearbox a new lease of life.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: David, Gill and their son Freddie hope Noel Fitzpatrick can help their Labrador Scooby, who has a life-threatening tumour in his leg. The vet goes to extraordinary lengths to save the life of Tiger the cat after his heart stops during his recovery from an operation, and when a lurcher called Otis brought in with shotgun wounds, Noel must extract the bullet before working out how to save the animal's leg. Plus, will a pair of tights help a French bulldog walk again?(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel transforms the attic into a studio and storage space for her vintage treasures and takes Dorothy on a trip to Paris, while Dick has his own plans for repurposing some ancient tools he finds in the outbuildings. The couple throws a surprise birthday celebration for Angel's mother, where Dick's purpose-built open-fire popcorn maker is put to the test.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of architect Richard Hawkes and his wife Sophie, as they move from London to the Kent countryside to build a hi-tech eco-friendly house with room to grow their own food. Richard also wants their new home to blend effortlessly into the surrounding countryside, concealed beneath a huge arch of clay tiles covered with earth and planted with grass.(n)

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Special episode of the documentary examining life in the two counties in south-west England. In Brixham, trawlerman Dave Driver sets out to sea on his boat, in search of cuttlefish.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Broadcasters Sue Perkins and Liza Tarbuck drive along the Ho Chi Minh Trail, a network of roads and paths in Southeast Asia that was littered with unexploded bombs following the Vietnam War. Their journey takes them through jungles and mountains to some of the least-explored corners of Laos and Vietnam, and proves to be a gruelling test of their bravery, motoring skills and senses of humour.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul

In his own kitchen on the Greek island of Symi, Rick cooks traditional Albanian dishes, including Lepur Comlek and Tave Kosi.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a project to build a car that can turn into a hovercraft and bring relief to flooded areas. Their efforts do not start smoothly, but it is not long before the trio are demonstrating the brilliance of their creation with an eventful journey down the River Avon. Plus, Jeremy grabs the keys to two variants of the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG supercar - the V8-engined Black Series with a roaring 622bhp, and the 740bhp Electric Drive, which is powered by four electric motors. X-Men and Les Miserables star Hugh Jackman is the latest celebrity to take the new Reasonably Priced Car around the test track.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from.(n)

programme.title: You've Got a Friend: The Carole King Story
programme.desc: The award-winning US singer gives an account of her life, looking back on her upbringing in Brooklyn, and the subsequent success she enjoyed as part of husband-and-wife songwriting team Goffin and King in the 1960s, including the creation of global hits Take Good Care of My Baby, The Locomotion and Will You Love Me Tomorrow. By 1970, Carole was divorced and had moved to Los Angeles, where she created her classic solo album Tapestry. Now in her seventies, she reveals herself to be as much an environmental activist as a musician, lobbying Congress in defence of the wildlife and eco-systems in her beloved Idaho.(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris takes a trip through India on the Konkan railway, a line that opened in 1998 and runs down the west coast. He starts his journey at dawn in the country's financial capital Mumbai, where the Mandovi Express is the first of many trains he catches along the 472 miles of track running through the states of Maharashtra, Goa and Karnataka. To keep the line straight, 92 tunnels and 2,000 bridges had to be built in one of the wettest areas of the subcontinent and Chris joins the monsoon team, which patrols daily to ensure that it is free from obstructions.(n)

programme.desc: Car restorer Wayne Carini finds out what happened to some of his past projects, including a Rolls-Royce that ended up at the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance charity event.(n)

programme.title: Chasing Classic Cars
programme.desc: Car restorer Wayne Carini is delighted to find a 1963 427 Cobra in a barn, and he and Roger manage to get it running and take it for a ride.(n)

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: After an injury to one of their members, the Young Guns face a tough decision on how to continue mining. Meanwhile, the Cheals break through to a long-lost underground mine.(n)

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: The crews and boats are feel the brunt of one of the hardest Opilio seasons on record, and it remains to be seen which of the captains will brave the conditions to haul their catch.(n)

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard finds a Devon salvage yard filled with interesting pieces. He also visits an inventor in Battle, East Sussex, whose collection includes a mummified cat.(n)

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Turbo embarks on a journey through an area affected by bush fires, and Yogi faces the biggest week of his career.(n)

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer travels to the US to hunt for a DeLorean DMC-12, a car that ceased production in 1982. Once he has acquired one, Edd China is given the task of restoring the stainless steel-clad vehicle, which was manufactured in Belfast.(n)

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters
programme.desc: The Bushmen combat opal thieves on land and in the skies. Plus, Lightning Ridge's Gavan McFarlane puts his son to work and the Boulder Boys dig their way out of trouble.(n)

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: At a Catholic church in Liverpool, Drew Pritchard's prayers are answered with a pair of very rare chairs. In Leicestershire, he finds industrial gems in the world's largest bell foundry.(n)

programme.title: Aussie Gold Hunters

programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: The gold-dredger fleet risks everything on new technology and fresh opportunities, and Vernon hunts for a new ship to launch his 'golden seahorse'.(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks up four mouth-watering recipes, kicking things off with a deliciously foolproof aromatic lamb curry that he knocks together in no time, followed by his flavour-packed harissa squash salad. Next up, Jamie cooks a sophisticated and speedy rosé pesto prawn pasta - an impressive dish that's deceptively easy to make. Saving the best for last, he also makes the fastest little flapjacks around - Buddy's flapjack biscuits.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog walking entrepreneur Jordanna hosts on the third night in and around Hull, hoping to impress her guests with a Mexican-themed dinner party.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The last night in Doncaster finds business manager Tony hosting with some countryside cuisine. Things don't quite go to plan when he has to stomach a large portion of blunt feedback about the authenticity of his locally sourced supper, dirty cutlery and gloopy gravy.(n)

programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon Garner and her son Shaun help husband-and-wife duo Mark and Sue Tracy from Manchester buy their first-ever property together, on a budget of 90,000 euros.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sophie and Manuel help a couple find a retirement home in Alicante, while in Almeria Steve and Shaun look for a holiday retreat perfect for a couple with a rescue dog.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Newlyweds with a love of dancing hunt for a home in Almeria, while in Alicante, a Liverpudlian couple want a house with plenty of outside space for the husband's potted plants.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses

Meanwhile, Hannah is capable of achieving top grades, but frequently finds excuses to leave class early.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Sauna
programme.desc: Candid documentary offering a glimpse at the secretive world of gay saunas, gaining access to an establishment in Nottingham where men go to meet strangers for sex. The programme meets people from a wide variety of backgrounds who frequent the sauna, from teachers and plumbers to Tory councillors, as well as hearing from the staff who work there, including owners Joe and John, who have been a couple for over 30 years. The programme provides a rarely before seen snapshot of gay culture, in a world where only men are welcome.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show Hertfordshire couple Phil and Geraldine properties in Devon and Bergerac, with a UK budget of £375,000 and £275,000 for France.(n)

programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton show Neil and Sarah Marks properties in Carcassonne and Wiltshire, as they look to settle down following Neil's retirement.(n)

Jonnie Irwin pulls out all the stops to keep them in beautiful Snowdonia with a budget of £300,000, while Laura Hamilton does everything she can to persuade Rich to buy in Granada with three stunning properties for up to £400,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Tom and Danielle Raffield, whose lifelong passion has been steam-bending wood. They've used the technique to create extraordinary furniture and lighting, but now plan to use it on a larger scale. In an attempt to escape the confines of their tiny gamekeeper's lodge, they have decided to build a wavy wooden house in South Cornwall, completed with curved cladding, twisted furniture and interior walls covered with weathered timber. With a £100,000 budget, the pair have decided to do a lot of the building themselves, and Kevin is on hand to discover whether they're up to the challenge.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with previous stories to find out what happened next. Pensioners John and Irene bought and rented out a house, only for their tenants to stop paying rent and move in their friends. The property ended up in a terrible state of repair - but worst of all, the landlords faced verbal threats and behaviour. Plus, the story of a tanning salon owner who offered an employee his home to rent, only for her to stop paying and turn it into a party house.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on stories featured in previous episodes, including Kent couple Rachel and Chris, who were pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant broke down and she stopped paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Plus, a young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby, and a landlord who reduced her tenants' rent when they got into financial difficulties - only for the payments to end altogether.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: An edition catching up with previous cases. Owed thousands in rent, landlord Matthew decided to evict his tenant, and called on an eviction specialist for help. As the tenant lived with seven cats, Matthew was worried about the state of the property, but that turned out to be the least of his problems. Cameras catch up with Matthew to see if he turned his property around. Having found a buyer for his house, Kirk asked the sitting tenant to leave. However, on the day of the move, she refused, stopped paying rent and Kirk's sale fell through. Kirk had to evict his nightmare tenant before he lost the house. Was he able to sell his property at least, or was he tempted to become a landlord once again?(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The efforts of north London landlord Franc to evict a tenant withholding rent payments on a property whose boiler mysteriously stopped working whenever the rent was due, and a tenant with asthma who has been forced to sleep on his sofa for two years in a damp flat. programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Enforcement officers are faced with the difficult job of evicting a woman with two children - a task that takes a turn for the worse when her ex-partner makes an appearance. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: The documentary examining the lives of individuals on the dole in the UK follows the stories of Roma people who moved to the UK and are now surviving on payments from the welfare state. Ion is from Romania and wants to make £40,000 from benefits to help him build a new house for his family back in his home village, while Katarina arrived from Slovakia seven years ago and has 11 children and 11 grandchildren living in and around an estate in Rotherham, South Yorkshire. Nottingham resident Viorel is 25 and lost both legs in an accident in his native Romania when he was seven, and now wants to pursue a career in the movies.(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces across the UK to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to fight the huge rise in street crime. In London, the Met's robbery squads work the streets on a busy Friday night, and with criminals now using motorbikes to get away, cameras help officers identify, track and apprehend offenders. Nick also finds out how pickpockets, scammers and bag thieves use sleight of hand and distraction to relieve people of their valuables.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Mind Over Mayhem (1974)
programme.desc: A scientist is overjoyed when his son follows in his footsteps, receiving widespread acclaim for a revolutionary new theory. When a fellow boffin produces evidence of plagiarism, the ruthless father resorts to murder to save his son from a scandal. But he reckons without the dishevelled detective, who sees through his efforts to make the killing look like the work of burglars. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Jose Ferrer and Lew Ayres.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Bird in the Hand (1992)
programme.desc: A compulsive gambler with a mountain of debts comes up with a plan to make some easy money. He plants a bomb under his wealthy uncle's car in the hope of inheriting a large legacy - but a hit-and-run driver jeopardises the entire scheme and Columbo is soon sniffing around for clues. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Tyne Daly, Greg Evigan and Don S Davis.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Prescription Murder (1968)
programme.desc: A psychiatrist having an affair with a patient murders his wife with his lover's help. First episode of the detective series, starring Peter Falk and Gene Barry.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Friend in Deed (1974)
programme.desc: A Californian police commissioner helps a rich, influential friend to conceal his complicity in murdering his own wife - but reckons without detective Columbo's timely intervention and his dogged determination to bring the truth to light. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Richard Kiley and Rosemary Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder with Too Many Notes (2000)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Grand Deceptions (1989)
programme.desc: An army colonel who embezzled money from his superior officer to fund arms sales becomes the target of a blackmail attempt when his crimes are discovered. programme.desc: Under suspension pending a psychiatric evaluation, Goren places his life and career in jeopardy by working undercover to take down a high-profile drug-dealer - and expose two corrupt police officers in the process. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Resident capuchin TJ causes mischief when he joins the new Chilean arrivals, and alpha male Paddy has to step in. Orang-utan Amy is pregnant but the keepers worry she will not be able to care for a baby, and chimps from Hananya's group get their teeth cleaned.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The team must make an important decision concerning the safety of a chimp group when alpha male Hananya continues to exhibit violent behaviour. Elsewhere, chimps Simon and Eveline compete in a swing-off, dental surgery is carried out and the park's staff prepare for the next woolly monkey birth.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A baby chimp tries to prove he is grown up enough to go it alone. The Bachelor Boys' reputation takes a knock when they become frightened of a new toy. Two of Hananya's charges pluck up the courage to explore their surroundings, and the Chilean capuchins get their first taste of life in the outside world.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The park hosts a morning sing-off, and golden-cheeked gibbon Zoey introduces a new member of the family. Over in Hananya's group, hot potatoes are on the menu, and a fight among the female capuchins leaves Phoebe with a bite wound that requires surgery.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Raisin
programme.desc: It's all about the quiche in this tasty whodunnit starring Ashley Jensen as Agatha Raisin, a PR whizz caught up in a murder mystery.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Staff members worry about the health of a newborn monkey, while infant orang-utan Dinda gets to grips with the outside enclosure, and stump-tailed macaque Koko has difficulties with her living accommodation.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The life of a baby woolly monkey hangs in the balance just 24 hours after its birth and Charlie is bullied by other chimps, meaning his days of living in the bachelor group could be numbered.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Chimp toddler Bryan finally makes friends with Seamus, the bad boy of the group - but it is not long before they fall out again.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A special edition featuring a project to build Monkey World's first foreign sanctuary for endangered primates, a rescue centre in Vietnam that aims to provide future protection for gibbons.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Work begins on the gibbon rescue centre in Vietnam, just as the first primates arrive to begin their rehabilitation. Back on home soil, Paddy's chimpanzee group welcomes a new arrival.(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A tense day of introductions lies in store for the chimpanzees and gibbons, and the team starts work on its biggest and most ambitious mission yet - trying to rescue almost 100 primates from Chile.(n)

programme.title: Blood & Treasure
programme.desc: Danny and Lexi follow a clue to the mountains of Spain in search of a lost train car that may have carried the sarcophagus of Cleopatra when it disappeared.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare UK
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall documentary series following the work of customs officers in the south of England as they tackle smugglers in ports, airports and at sea.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Diane Downs, the Arizona-born mother of three who in 1983 arrived at hospital, having murdered one of her children and left her remaining two critically injured.(n)

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: The exploits of Scottish murderer and trickster Archibald Hall, who posed as a butler to steal from his employers, and killed a felon who threatened to reveal his criminal past. He later murdered a wealthy former MP and his wife after they had employed his services and was finally arrested for his crimes in January 1978.(n)

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: Fifty-seven-year-old Ella Douglas went missing in 2000. This documentary examines whether the suspicious behaviour of an old flame holds the key to what happened to her.(n)

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: Northumbria Police answer a flood of 999 calls, while officers raid a property they suspect is being used by men to exploit young girls.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Former drug-smuggler Eddie Padilla shares the story of how he managed to escape from one of Peru's most notorious prisons despite being told he would be killed by the guards if he attempted it. Nicknamed the House of the Devil, he and two friends were placed in the Lurigancho Prison after a run-in with the police while on a smuggling trip.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of Vietnam Prisoners of War Ernie Brace and John McCain and the friendship they formed by tapping code through the cell walls of Hanoi Hilton prison in the late 1960s. The pair did not meet face to face until an event at the White House in 1973. Brace was the longest-held American civilian prisoner, detained for nearly eight years. McCain would go on to become a US senator and contest the Republican nomination for president against George W Bush.(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Chicago Outfit enforcer Frank Cullotta discusses crime boss Tony Spilotro, including his life in the mob and the burglary that led to his capture.(n)

programme.title: Border Security: Canada's Front Line
programme.desc: The work of officers of the Canada Border Services Agency as its staff enforce customs, quarantine, immigration and finance laws. programme.title: Border Security: Canada's Front Line
programme.desc: The work of officers of the Canada Border Services Agency as its staff enforce customs, quarantine, immigration and finance laws. programme.title: UK Border Force
programme.desc: Documentary series taking viewers inside the UK Border Agency, revealing the work that takes place behind the scenes of the immigration process. Teams at Heathrow Terminal 3, Calais, Dover and across the North West and North East of England reveal the methods and technology used to allow legitimate visitors safe passage while catching those who try to gain illegal entry to the UK.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Evil sorceress Morgana summons mighty gatekeeper the Cailleach, who opens a door to another world from which hellish creatures emerge. As Arthur and his loyal knights take up arms, Merlin discovers the only thing that will stop them is an unimaginable sacrifice. Colin Morgan returns as the young wizard in the first of a two-part story, guest starring Gemma Jones (the Harry Potter movies) and Emilia Fox (Silent Witness).(n)

programme.title: Double Murder: The Inside Story
programme.desc: Documentary following officers as they investigate the murders of two Polish brothers in 2005, from the moment the bodies are discovered in Luton to the culprits' arrest.(n)

programme.title: Killer Colleagues
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a horse trainer in a small seaside town in northern England. The chief suspect tuned out to be a scorned ex-employee she had replaced.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: A rollercoaster car comes off the tracks and lands in a car park, killing six people. Sara examines the rail and finds the incident was an act of sabotage. The case becomes more complicated with the discovery that one of the apparent victims actually died 16 hours before the disaster. Meanwhile, Warrick and Catherine investigate the murder of a 13-year-old girl.(n)

programme.title: Royal Cousins at War
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining what impact the relationships between cousins Nicholas II of Russia, Kaiser Wilhelm II of Germany and George V of the United Kingdom had on the outbreak of the First World War. This episode focuses on the story of the emerging divisions and rivalries between the inter-related royal houses of Europe during the 19th century. Featuring contributions by historians Karina Urbach, Miranda Carter and Piers Brendon. Tamsin Greig narrates.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed

programme.title: The Repo Man

programme.title: Brothers' Day (2015)

Fact-based crime drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Jim Sturgess and Sam Worthington.(n)

programme.desc: An actor's career collapses after a series of mistakes, and ends up an online laughing stock when his ex-girlfriend leaks their sex tape. Romantic comedy, starring Ray Panthaki and Daisy Bevan.(n)

programme.title: Royal Cousins at War
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines the key role played by royalty in smoothing the path to the Anglo-Russian entente of 1907, and the part played by the Kaiser's erratic, unstable personality in the growing isolation of Germany in the years leading up to 1914. The film also explores the roles played by the German, British and Russian monarchs in the months leading up to the outbreak of the First World War and tells the story of George V's refusal to grant his cousin Nicholas II asylum in England following the Russian Revolution of 1917.(n)

programme.title: Royal Cousins at War
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how European royal families ended up on opposite sides during the First World War despite ties of blood and marriage. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)
